exercises.title: Signature Size Impact Analysis
exercises.description: Calculate the impact of signature sizes on real-world protocols.
tasks: Calculate total signature bytes for a 3-certificate TLS chain (FN-DSA-512, ML-DSA-44, SLH-DSA-128s)
Calculate bandwidth overhead for 1 million TLS handshakes per day with each algorithm
Estimate storage requirements for 1 billion signatures with each algorithm
Calculate the ratio improvement of FN-DSA over alternatives
Identify scenarios where FN-DSA's size advantage is most significant
exercises.expectedOutput: Comprehensive size impact analysis with concrete numbers
hints: TLS chain typically has 3 signatures: root, intermediate, leaf
Consider both storage and bandwidth costs

exercises.title: NIST PQC Portfolio Comparison
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive comparison of all NIST PQC signature standards.
tasks: Create a table comparing ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and FN-DSA across all parameters
Map each algorithm to its best use cases
Identify the unique advantage of each algorithm
Explain why NIST standardized all three rather than just one
Design a decision tree for algorithm selection
exercises.expectedOutput: Comprehensive comparison document with decision guidance
hints: Consider: size, speed, security assumptions, implementation complexity

exercises.title: NTRU vs Module-LWE Comparison
exercises.description: Research and compare the mathematical foundations of NTRU and Module-LWE.
tasks: Describe the NTRU problem and its history (since 1990s)
Describe the Module-LWE problem and its history
Compare cryptanalysis history of both problems
Explain why using both provides algorithm diversity
Research any known attacks specific to each
exercises.expectedOutput: Research report on lattice problem comparison
hints: NTRU was proposed by Hoffstein, Pipher, Silverman in 1996
LWE was proposed by Regev in 2005
Consider both classical and quantum attacks
Look at best known attack complexities

exercises.title: Implementation Complexity Assessment
exercises.description: Assess the implementation complexity of FN-DSA vs ML-DSA.
tasks: List the mathematical operations required for FN-DSA signing
List the mathematical operations required for ML-DSA signing
Identify operations requiring floating-point arithmetic
Assess side-channel vulnerability surface for each
Recommend which is better for embedded systems
hints: FN-DSA: FFT, Gaussian sampling, polynomial operations
ML-DSA: NTT, rejection sampling, modular arithmetic
Floating-point can leak information through timing/cache
